source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
In the diagram on the right we see the birdô-eye view and front elevation of a solid that is defined by flat surfaces (i.e. view from obove and the front respectively). Bird' s-Eye View (view from above): <image1>. Front Elevation (view from the front): <image2>.
Which of the outlines I to IV can be the side elevation (i.e. view from the left) of the same object?
<image3>

image_dependency: Question explicitly says 'In the diagram' and references <image1>, <image2>, and <image3>.
Task requires matching bird's-eye view and front elevation to a valid side elevation, which is inherently visual/spatial.
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the item is not solvable from text alone.
multi_step: Solving requires integrating at least two projections of the same solid (top view and front view) to infer a third projection (left side view).
The object is defined by flat surfaces, so the solver must reason across multiple spatial constraints rather than read off a single visible fact.
Multiple candidate outlines I to IV must be compared against the inferred side elevation, adding a selection/elimination step.
verifiability: Answer is a discrete multiple-choice outcome: answer_text='IV' with choices provided.
The correctness target is unambiguous and easy to compare against the final prediction.
registry_passed=true and answer_present=true support basic asset/answer availability, though image reference issues reduce confidence somewhat.

The item is highly image-dependent, but the question references <image1>, <image2>, and <image3> while only one top-level image asset is registered, creating asset completeness risk.

Because the sample appears usable but has unresolved visual asset issues, it should enter the candidate pool at lower priority rather than be rejected.
